questions.question: What does border-collapse: collapse do?
options: Merges adjacent cell borders into one
questions.explanation: border-collapse: collapse merges the borders of adjacent cells into a single border.

questions.question: How do you create zebra-striped table rows?
questions.explanation: tr:nth-child(even) selects even-numbered rows for zebra striping.

questions.question: How do you make a table responsive on mobile?
options: Set width: 100% and overflow-x: auto on wrapper
questions.explanation: Wrap the table in a container with overflow-x: auto and set width: 100% on the table for horizontal scrolling on small screens.

questions.question: What does table-layout: fixed do?
options: Sets column widths based on the first row rather than content
Prevents the table from being resized
Fixes the table position on the page
questions.explanation: table-layout: fixed forces column widths based on the first row or specified widths, improving performance.

questions.question: Which HTML element represents the table header?
questions.explanation: <th> (table header) defines a header cell, typically rendered bold and centered by default.

questions.question: What does border-spacing control?
options: The space between table cells when borders are separate
The space between table rows only
questions.explanation: border-spacing sets the distance between cell borders when border-collapse is separate (not collapsed).

questions.question: How do you style table rows on hover?
questions.explanation: tr:hover applies styles when any part of a table row is hovered over.

questions.question: What does the <caption> element do?
options: Adds a title or description to a table
Adds a header row to the table
questions.explanation: <caption> provides a title or description for a table, similar to a figcaption for images.

questions.question: What is the difference between auto and fixed table layout?
options: auto adjusts columns based on content; fixed uses first row or explicit widths
fixed is faster but may overflow; auto is slower but respects content
auto is only for responsive tables
questions.explanation: table-layout: auto adjusts column widths based on content (slower). fixed uses first-row widths (faster, more predictable).

questions.question: How do you add padding inside table cells?
options: Use padding on td and th elements
questions.explanation: CSS padding on td and th creates internal spacing in table cells, preferred over the HTML cellpadding attribute.

questions.question: What does empty-cells: hide do?
options: Hides all empty cells in a table
Hides borders and background of empty cells
Removes empty cells from the layout
Hides the table if it's empty
questions.explanation: empty-cells: hide hides the borders and background of cells with no content when border-collapse is separate.

questions.question: How do you align text to the top of a table cell?
questions.explanation: vertical-align (not text-align) controls the vertical positioning of content within table cells.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the scope attribute on <th>?
options: Defines whether the header applies to a row or column for accessibility
Sets the scope of table sorting
questions.explanation: scope="col" or scope="row" on <th> associates headers with data cells, helping screen readers navigate tables.

questions.question: What does the <colgroup> element do?
options: Groups columns for styling without applying to each cell
Creates a group of table cells
questions.explanation: <colgroup> with <col> elements allows applying styles (like background) to entire columns at once.

questions.question: How do you create a full-width table?
questions.explanation: width: 100% on the table element stretches it to fill the full width of its container.

questions.question: What is the default border-collapse value?
questions.explanation: The default border-collapse value is 'separate', meaning each cell has its own borders with space between them.

questions.question: How can you highlight the first column of a table?
questions.explanation: td:first-child or the <col> element with :first-child can be used to style the first column.

questions.question: What is the recommended approach for sorting indicators in tables?
options: Use CSS arrows/rotated icons to show sort direction
Sorting can't be indicated with CSS alone
questions.explanation: CSS can add arrows or rotate icons to indicate sort direction, often with ::after pseudo-elements on sortable headers.

questions.question: What does vertical-align: middle do in a table cell?
options: Aligns content to the middle of the cell vertically
Centers the cell in the row
Aligns the cell in the middle of the table
questions.explanation: vertical-align: middle centers the content vertically within the table cell.

questions.question: How do you style thead, tbody, and tfoot differently?
questions.explanation: CSS selectors for thead, tbody, and tfoot elements allow separate styling for header, body, and footer sections.
